##NAMES=Fluormacraeite
##RRUFFID=R250077
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=[(H_2_O)K]Mn_2_(Fe_2_Ti)(PO_4_)_4_(OF)(H_2_O)_10_·4H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Plößberg pegmatite, Upper Palatinate, Bavaria, Germany
##OWNER=Mineralogical State Collection, Munich
##SOURCE=Ian Grey
##DESCRIPTION=Isolated pale-yellow rhombic tablets associated with spherical blue phosphosiderite, pink-coloured strengite micro-crystals, white fluorapatite globules, light-yellow leucophosphite, black–green rockbridgeite, and reddish-brown cacoxenite. Type sample, described in Grey et al (2025)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250077
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=[K_0.14_(H_2_O)_0.76_][K_0.79_(H_2_O)_0.21_](Mn^2+^_1.75_Mg_0.25_)(Fe^3+^_1.84_Al_0.13_Ti^4+^_1.02_Mg_0.01_)(PO_4_)_4.00_[O_0.94_F_0.81_(OH)_0.25_](H_2_O)_10_·3.90H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder, profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 10.531 b: 20.652 c: 12.408 alpha: 90 beta: 90.1 gamma: 90 volume: 2699 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
